Market Intelligence Brief
Generated 10:06 ET
Massive index put hedging dominates as negative gamma amplifies pre-NFP positioning
KEY TAKEAWAYS
  • 1. The 126K SPY put block trade at $653-654 strikes signals institutional hedging ahead of tomorrow's NFP print with dealers short $5.9B gamma to amplify any directional move.
  • 2. Single-name call flow (+340% UNIT, +123% NKE) diverges sharply from index put demand, creating a classic dispersion setup into volatile employment data.
  • 3. Vol backwardation across 19 names including AAPL and AMD suggests traders are pricing elevated risk through the jobs report, with negative gamma ensuring outsized reactions.
NARRATIVE — pre-NFP defensive positioning

Index put demand overwhelmed single-name positioning as traders braced for tomorrow's NFP report, with the most telling signal being 126K combined SPY put contracts at the $653-654 strikes — effectively a $8.2B notional hedge at current spot of $654.04. The index P/C ratio of 1.55 versus single-name P/C of 1.18 creates a stark divergence, while dealers sitting on $5.9B negative gamma in SPY ensure any NFP surprise gets amplified beyond typical moves. What stands out is the two-way flow in NKE (+123% calls, +109% puts) driving total volume +216% higher, likely tied to positioning around the company's innovation strategy refresh rather than pure directional bets. Meanwhile, UNIT exploded +340% on call volume and PLAB saw +422% put demand, though both lack obvious news catalysts suggesting either earnings whispers or sector rotation plays ahead of the jobs data. The vol surface confirms defensive positioning with 19 names in backwardation including mega-cap AAPL, AMD, and ARM, while implied correlation at 0.21 signals stock-specific dispersion opportunities. Rich vol names like DASH (+146% vs sector) and cheap vol names like COST (-36% vs sector) set up clear relative value trades, though the negative gamma environment means any dispersion trades require tight risk management into Friday's employment print.

Layer 1: Sentiment -17Layer 2: SPY GEX -$5.9BLayer 3: Index P/C 1.55Layer 9: NFP tomorrow
